title: rosa eglanteria var. spineourceolata
intro: Rosa eglanteria var. spineourceolata, also known as the prickly rose or Rosa spinosissima, is a species of flowering plant in the rose family. It is a perennial shrub with prickly stems and fragrant white flowers, traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties. The plant is said to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may provide health benefits such as reducing fever, soothing digestive issues, and promoting wound healing. Rosa eglanteria var. spineourceolata can be prepared as herbal teas, infusions, or dried flowers for medicinal use, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and optimal preparations.
